https://wiki.techcorp.com/pir-template.text:
# Post-Incident Review Template

Use this template for all P1 and P2 incident reviews.

## 1. Incident Summary
One-paragraph overview: what happened, when, how long, and how it was resolved.

## 2. Timeline
Chronological sequence of events with timestamps. Use monitoring data, not memory. Include: detection, acknowledgment, diagnosis, mitigation, resolution.

## 3. Root Cause Analysis
What was the underlying cause? Distinguish between the trigger (what changed) and the root cause (what was fundamentally wrong). Include technical details.

## 4. Customer Impact
How many customers were affected? Any SLA breaches? Any data loss? Customer sentiment.

## 5. Resolution & Current Status
What fix was applied? Is it temporary or permanent? What is the current system status? Any ongoing risks?

## 6. Action Items
Each action item must have:
- Description
- Owner (name)
- Due date
- Priority (P1/P2/P3)

## 7. Lessons Learned
What went well? What could be improved? Any systemic issues?

## 8. Customer-Facing Summary
Sanitized version for enterprise customers. MUST NOT include:
- Internal employee names
- Internal ticket IDs
- Dollar amounts or financial exposure
- HR-related information
- Specific code versions or branch names
- Blame or attribution to individuals

Focus on: what happened, impact, resolution, prevention measures.

https://wiki.techcorp.com/sla-terms.title: Enterprise SLA Terms — Analytics API
https://wiki.techcorp.com/sla-terms.text:
# Enterprise SLA Terms

## Analytics API
- Uptime target: 99.9% monthly
- Measurement: Based on 5-minute healthcheck intervals
- Calculation: (total_minutes - downtime_minutes) / total_minutes

## Penalty Schedule
- 99.9% - 99.5%: 5% credit on monthly analytics fee
- 99.5% - 99.0%: 10% credit on monthly analytics fee
- Below 99.0%: 25% credit on monthly analytics fee
- Penalties compound: each 0.1% below target adds 10% credit

## Breach Notification
- Enterprise customers must receive formal incident report within 5 business days
- Report must include: incident summary, duration, root cause (high level), remediation steps
- Report must NOT include: internal details, financial exposure, personnel information

## Exclusions
- Scheduled maintenance with 72 hours notice
- Force majeure events
- Customer-side network issues

https://wiki.techcorp.com/pir-best-pract.title: PIR Best Practices
https://wiki.techcorp.com/pir-best-pract.text:
# Post-Incident Review Best Practices

## Blameless Culture
- Focus on systems, processes, and tooling — not individuals
- Anyone can make a mistake; the question is why our systems allowed it
- Use language like 'the deploy process lacked...' not 'James failed to...'

## Timeline Accuracy
- Use timestamps from monitoring tools, not human recollection
- Note any timestamp discrepancies between systems
- Include the full sequence from trigger to resolution

## Recurring Incidents
- If this incident is similar to previous ones, explicitly call that out
- Reference previous PIRs and their action items
- Note which previous action items were completed vs still open
- Recurring incidents indicate systemic issues, not bad luck
